text.title: Playwright Agent Workflow and Gotchas
text.summary: Agentic browser automation workflow, playwright-mcp integration, gotchas, and services for hosted browser infrastructure.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: A browser agent gets a task ("scrape product table from URL X" or "verify checkout flow"), generates a Playwright script using role-based locators, runs headless with --trace=on-first-retry, and on failure attaches the trace + screenshot to the task report. For E2E: use Playwright's test runner. For one-off scrapes: use a plain node script.mjs.

rule.statement: For agentic web tasks, prefer Microsoft playwright-mcp (MCP server) over hand-rolled scripts — it gives the LLM accessibility-tree snapshots which are far more reliable than raw DOM dumps.
rule.rationale: DOM dumps from page.content() are huge and noisy (scripts, styles, hidden elements). The accessibility tree contains only what users can interact with, dramatically reducing token consumption and hallucination risk.

section.reference: Headless scrape script with auth reuse via storageState
Prompt for generating TypeScript Playwright tests with role-locator rules

antipattern.reason: Ban it via CI lint rule. Adds 2 seconds per occurrence, scales with suite size, and masks actual timing problems. Use locator auto-wait or expect(locator).toBeVisible({timeout}).

antipattern.description: Agents broadening selectors when getByRole() throws "strict mode violation: multiple elements".
antipattern.reason: The correct fix is narrowing with { name: 'specific label' }. Broadening picks an arbitrary element and silently tests the wrong thing.
auth.json contains session tokens. Add to .gitignore and pull from secret store at test time. Trace artifacts may also contain PII; gate behind staging-only.

rule.statement: Review the first generated test before scaling to full suite. Fixing 200 brittle CSS selectors after the fact is worse than reviewing 1 test before it becomes the template.
rule.rationale: Agent-generated test suites clone patterns from the first test. A bad first test produces 200 copies of the same bad pattern.

section.title: Hosted Browser Services
section.p: Browserbase: SaaS, Playwright CDP API, stealth modes for bot-detection avoidance.
Browserless.io: SaaS + OSS, self-hostable, ideal for headless at scale.
Microsoft Playwright Testing: cloud parallel runs from CI.
Argos: visual diff SaaS for Playwright screenshots, OSS + SaaS.

section.title: CI Fan-Out
section.p: Use --shard=1/4 (--shard=2/4 etc.) in CI matrix for parallel fan-out across workers. With 4 shards, a 10-minute suite runs in ~2.5 minutes.
